---
title: "Data Science Learnings"
author: "Nicholas Huang"
date: "2025-08-18"
categories: learning
image: "Learnings.jpg"
---

Things that I've learnt.

**Exploratory Data Analysis:**
- Using df.info()/df.describe()/df.head() to have an initial look at the data 
- To use "Hue" parameter in a pairplot to show trends in the target variable'


**Preprocessing:**
- Different ways of different variable types: select_dtypes and make_column_selector
- Using the combination of make_column_transform and make_pipeline to transform features for the model

**Concepts:**
Overfitting - Models that are too complex for the data, captures a lot of the noise in the model leading to poor generalisation, shown by the testing error being much higher than the training error. Ca
Underfitting - Models that are too simple for the data, doesn't capture much noise which leads to poor accuracy
Bayes Error Rate - The error of the best model with unlimited samples
Regularisation - Favouring simpler models over more complex models
Inductive/Learning Bias - Set of assumptions a learner uses to predict outputs based on inputs unseen
Variance - Error due to high sensitivity to variation in the training set 
Bias Error - Error due to erroneous assumptions in the learner

**Metrics:**
- Using a baseline model like DummyClassifier/DummyRegressor to compare model preformance can be useful

**Miscellaneous:**
- Convention to use underscore "_" as the name for a throwaway/unused variable
- In an interactive environment like a Jupyter Notebook, you can use the question mark after an object to show documentation/docstring e.g "StandardScaler?"
